title: Data Analysis 101
content:
Dealing with data requires several skills. Starting from collecting the data, analyzing it, and finally interpreting the data. The equipment needed to collect the data had to be calibrated first to acquire accurate results. Error analysis still needs to be performed to ensure the data's certainty. The method used to analyze the error, such as root mean square error (RMSE), calculates the percentage of error across all data obtained from the equipment.
Second, in data analysis several processes need to proceed before. data preprocessing, including data cleaning, or just simply move or convert the data into the required format. This process can't be underestimated. 
I think this process needed to be done correctly before further analysis. Depending on the software/tools where you want to process the data in a statistical manner, the data format should be prepared accordingly. 
After preprocessing, the next step is a simple statistical analysis to determine the pattern of the data.
Mean, median, and standard deviation are the most common variables in this step. Mean usually indicates the range in which the data is most varied. It can show us useful information, but if anything happens, like outliers, this information should be considered again. 
Median is the middle value of all data. And the most important is standard deviation. This value indicates that quartiles 1, 2, and 3 are also needed for the analysis.
The next level of analysis is to get the result if we want to proceed with the analysis based on the goal. 
Regression, hypothesis test, or classification. 
Regression is used to predict something based on the known variable. This prediction can be used as a tool to obtain future results based on the past data. Or else, to get the relationship between variables. 
Hypothesis tests (like T-tests) are useful to know the relationship between 2 or more variables, if one variable is statistically significant to another or not. Sometimes it can also be used to compare before and after data. 

A classification test is used to classify the data according to the pattern or standard needed, usually with some range of value produced by other standards.

These data analyses should be performed and visualized in a way that can be understood easily. So, data interpretation and visualization are the next steps after data analysis. 
Bar charts, scatter plots, pie charts, line charts, and histograms are the common ways to show data and are easily interpreted. The way the chart showed also affected the result. Color, consistency of the format, font, and another detail in the chart can easily affect the reader. 

Data analysis can be done using several software programs. The most popular one is Microsoft Excel; the next popular is SPSS (IBM). But I personally prefer Python libraries like Pandas, Matplotlib, or NumPy to do the analysis for me. 
It all depends on what kind of data you need to analyze and which skills you have. It is easy to start with Mic. Excel is very user-friendly; the tools are easy, and the formula is simple. But the visualization is limited but very advantageous in the preprocessing time. 

In conclusion, data analysis consists of data collection, data preprocessing, data analysis, and data visualization. Each step should be done correctly in the right manner.

title: If I’m starting AI as a complete beginner, these are the tools I should use and the best way to approach learning them
content:
Starting to study for a PhD in my early 30s means I have to catch up with any new technology that could help me navigate my own way. I have to familiarize myself with artificial intelligence applications in my field as soon as possible. 
Meanwhile, I have zero programming skills and just start from total scratch. 

This method is my best way of approaching if I have to restart my AI learning journey. 
1. Start with the basic tools.
Honestly, I started learning programming in MATLAB, and then I slowly started to learn the Python language. And as we know, Python is the main language in AI. 

2. Use simple tools :
I would recommend using Jupyter Notebook or Google Colab if you want to start your first code. These two are quite easy to follow and use. 

3. Learn machine learning before learning deep learning. 
Data processing, statistical learning, regression, and classification are the main basic features that you need to study or get used to. 

4. Practice with a small project. 
I started using Kaggle and saw how other people do the project using a simple dataset. Then try to do my own small project using the simple machine learning mode that I study. It never hurt to start small and develop your skill. 

There are so many AI LLMs like ChatGPT or Claude or even Gemini that can help you explain and simplify things. Read and make sure you understand the things that you look for.
